verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted protein (signal peptide, no TM domain, ER/Golgi-routed) with reversible extracellular function in seminal plasma and reproductive-tract fluid. No documented covalent or wash-resistant surface anchoring to a TM partner. No cancer-ecto, cell-death, lysosomal-exocytosis, dual-PM-cycling, or tissue-restricted-surface-display pools. Reversible binding to sperm/egg surface receptors is equilibrium recruitment, not stable surface residency; the bound solute is in rapid exchange with the free pool. Testis/reproductive-tissue restriction reflects tissue-specific synthesis/secretion, not surface-display biology.
